name: Microsoft Teams Channel
description: Connect Row-Bot to Microsoft Teams as a bot channel.
long_description: Receive and send Microsoft Teams bot messages through Row-Bot, including streaming-style updates, approvals, pairing, slash commands, and supported personal-chat attachments.

bot_app_id.label: Microsoft App ID
bot_app_id.description: Application/client ID for the Azure Bot or Microsoft 365 app bot.

authority_tenant.label: Token Authority Tenant
authority_tenant.description: Use botframework.com for multi-tenant Bot Framework bots, or your tenant ID for single-tenant apps.

allowed_tenant_ids.label: Allowed Teams Tenant IDs
allowed_tenant_ids.description: Optional newline-separated Microsoft Entra tenant IDs allowed to use this bot.

auth_mode.label: User Authorization Mode
auth_mode.description: Controls which Teams users may talk to Row-Bot.

single_user_id.label: Single Teams User ID
single_user_id.description: Optional Teams user ID for single-user mode.

default_target.label: Default Conversation ID
default_target.description: Optional Teams conversation ID used by generated send_teams_* tools.

require_mention_in_channels.label: Require Mention In Channels
require_mention_in_channels.description: Ignore team channel messages unless the bot is mentioned.

enable_streaming.label: Enable Streaming Updates
enable_streaming.description: Send a placeholder and update it while Row-Bot works.

auto_start_tunnel.label: Auto-start Row-Bot Tunnel
auto_start_tunnel.description: When enabled, Row-Bot may start its configured tunnel to expose the Teams webhook.

public_base_url.label: Public Base URL
public_base_url.description: Optional public Row-Bot URL if you manage your own tunnel or reverse proxy.

bot_client_secret.label: Bot Client Secret
bot_client_secret.description: Client secret/password for the bot app registration.
